subject: LU-136 use vmalloc() to allocate memory for sbi->s_group_desc

messages.message:
Patch Set 1:

Build Started http://newbuild.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/471/

Patch Set 1: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://newbuild.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/471/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1:

Builds with "--enable-ext4":

http://newbuild.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/473/

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(2 inline comments)

With this minor change to use ext4_msg() the patch should be submitted to the linux-ext4 mailing list.  Please change the commit message slightly to avoid mentioning RHEL5 and add the signed-off by lines:

Large kmalloc() for the group descriptor buffer pointers can fail for large filesystems, which will cause the "not enough memory" error while mounting.  Fall back to vmalloc() if the kmalloc() failed, as was done for s_flex_groups.

Signed-off-by: Yu Jian <yujian@whamcloud.com>
Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Patch Set 1: (2 inline comments)
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2: Looks good to me, approved

Patch Set 2: Fails

After installing the ext4 build, formatting an 199TB LUN failed with test node rebooted:

# mkfs.lustre --reformat --fsname=largefs --ost --mgsnode=192.168.77.1@o2ib --mkfsoptions='-t ext4 -i 1058576' --mountfsoptions='errors=remount-ro,extents,mballoc,force_over_16tb' /dev/md0

   Permanent disk data:
Target:     largefs-OSTffff
Index:      unassigned
Lustre FS:  largefs
Mount type: ldiskfs
Flags:      0x72
              (OST needs_index first_time update )
Persistent mount opts: errors=remount-ro,extents,mballoc,force_over_16tb
Parameters: mgsnode=192.168.77.1@o2ib

device size = 209076210MB
2 6 18

formatting backing filesystem ldiskfs on /dev/md0
        target name  largefs-OSTffff
        4k blocks     53523509760
        options       -t ext4 -i 1058576 -J size=400 -I 256 -q -O dir_index,extents,uninit_groups -F

mkfs_cmd = mke2fs -j -b 4096 -L largefs-OSTffff -t ext4 -i 1058576 -J size=400 -I 256 -q -O dir_index,extents,uninit_groups -F /dev/md0 53523509760

Connection to localhost closed by remote host.

Connection to localhost closed.

Syslog only showed:

May 16 00:43:14 localhost kernel: init dynlocks cache
May 16 00:43:14 localhost kernel: ldiskfs created from ext4-2.6-rhel5

Patch Set 2:

There is still a limitation of 128TB in the above codes.

While formatting an 129TB LUN by mkfs.lustre, after running the following code, the test node was rebooted somehow:

sbi->s_group_desc[i] = sb_bread(sb, block);

i=12796
block=12797

However, while formatting an 128TB LUN, the above codes would be run successfully with:

i=16383
block=16384

Andreas, could you please give me some suggestions about why there is a 128TB limitation here and how to fix it? Thanks a lot.

Patch Set 2: No score

(1 inline comment)

Please look at the output of "dumpe2fs" for both the 128TB and 129TB filesystems, to check what the block offset is for the group numbers that are causing problems.  The group number is likely (block_nr * (4096 / 64)).  There are 1M groups in a 128TB filesystem.

There shouldn't be any problem from reading blocks over 16384 for the 129TB filesystem, so I guess the problem is probably in the allocation of the s_group_desc array, because it will likely be using kmalloc() for <= 128TB filesystems and vmalloc() for > 128TB filesystems.

I can't imagine why this code is actually failing, but it is probably related to the access of vmalloc() memory above 128TB.

Is it possible to test this on RHEL6?  That kernel should allow larger kmalloc() allocations above 128kB.

Patch Set 1:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 5.6/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/fedc34ea-8138-11e0-b4df-52540025f9af).  Ran 1 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 2: Looks good to me, approved

I'm going to give this a +2, because it turns out there is nothing wrong with this patch itself, just that there are more bugs that need fixing in the ext4 code to handle filesystems over 128TB (or possibly smaller in case of fragmented free memory).

It might make sense to merge the other patches with this one, just to keep the "over 128TB" fixes in one commit, but I just wanted to mark that there is nothing wrong with this patch.

Patch Set 2: Looks good to me, approved

looks OK

Patch Set 2:

A modified version of this patch was landed upstream for ext4.  Should this kind of fix be landed for b1_8, or should the patch be abandoned?

Patch Set 2:

Per LU-477, this patch is inadequate for supporting >=128TB LUN. Commit e2d082e (http://git.whamcloud.com/?p=fs/lustre-release.git;a=commitdiff;h=e2d082e) is the full patch for master branch. If we're not going to guarantee supporting >=128TB LUN in the current b1_8 branch, then we could just abandon this patch. Johann, what's your opinion?

Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Let's not take the risk to land it on b1_8 for now.
Patch Set 2: Abandoned

commit.subject: LU-136 use vmalloc() to allocate memory for sbi->s_group_desc
commit.message:
LU-136 use vmalloc() to allocate memory for sbi->s_group_desc

Large kmalloc() for the group descriptor buffer pointers can fail
for large filesystems, which will cause the "not enough memory" error
while mounting. Fall back to vmalloc() if the kmalloc() failed, as
was done for s_flex_groups.

Signed-off-by: Yu Jian <yujian@whamcloud.com>
Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: If5d6b3e75cd51739b73858950da0b8440a26ee61
